image:image.image:caption: Whatever it is, the way you tell your story online can make all the difference.

image:image.image:caption: Mary speaking at a panel event at COP29

image:image.image:title: News - Holodomor Memorial Service - Make it stand out
image:image.image:caption: Whatever it is, the way you tell your story online can make all the difference.

image:image.image:title: News - Mary Meets Foxford School Council Leaders for Parliament Week - Make it stand out
image:image.image:caption: Mary with staff and students from Foxford Community School

image:image.image:title: News - Mary Visits The UK’s Second Oldest Emmaus Community - Make it stand out
image:image.image:caption: Mary with Tony Bivens, Senior Support Officer, and Falishia Channer, Community Director, at Emmaus Coventry and Warwickshire.

image:image.image:title: News - The Future Is Electric! - Make it stand out
image:image.image:caption: Mary with Richard Ball, Kate Reynolds, Ian Allder and Ali Yarr from Zenobe Energy

image:image.image:title: News - Tackling Violence Against Women - Make it stand out
image:image.image:caption: Mary meets with the CRASAC team: Mary Hawkins, Jenni Musket, Natalie Thompson and Rachel Hind.

image:image.image:title: News - DNA Codes A Big Win At COP16 - Make it stand out
image:image.image:caption: Mary and Steve Reed, the Secretary of State for DEFRA, in Cali, Colombia

image:image.image:title: News - Mary To Ban Single-Use Vapes - Make it stand out
image:image.image:caption: Disposable Vapes will no longer be sold in the UK after 1 June 2025

image:image.image:title: News - Mary Represents The UK At Visit To The United Nations General Assembly - Make it stand out
image:image.image:caption: Mary meets French President Emmanuel Macron

image:image.image:title: News - The Mersey Forest Gains 19,000 Trees - Make it stand out
image:image.image:caption: Mary meets Paul Nolan, CEO of the Mersey Forest

image:image.image:title: COP30 Coverage - Thursday 20 November - Make it stand out
image:image.image:caption: Despite the fire, Minister Creagh, Minister Miliband, and the negotiating teams were hard at work, finalising the historic COP30 agreements.

image:image.image:caption: Whatever it is, the way you tell your story online can make all the difference.

image:image.image:title: Home
image:image.image:caption: Mary, wearing an orange blouse, being sworn in as an MP in the House of Commons. She stands in front of the green leather benches and is holding a bible as she declares her the oath to the Deputy Speaker.

image:image.image:title: Home
image:image.image:caption: Mary, in a blue business suit, speaking from the front bench in the House of Commons.

image:image.image:title: About
image:image.image:caption: Mary wearing a red coat smiling and talking with a paramedic wearing his green uniform and with "Ambulance" on his back in a bright, modern hospital building.

image:image.image:title: About
image:image.image:caption: Mary standing in front of West Midlands Police station, wearing a blue suit with her arms folded.

image:image.image:title: About
image:image.image:caption: Three people in a subway station engaging with wall art; one is in a wheelchair with colorful butterfly designs on the wheels.

image:image.image:title: About
image:image.image:caption: Mary sitting on a chair looking through a photo album, wearing a floral blouse and dark blazer, near a window.

image:image.image:title: About
image:image.image:caption: Mary, wearing an orange high-visibility vest chatting to a group of people wearing high-visibility vests and hard hats at a construction site.

image:image.image:title: About
image:image.image:caption: Smiling person with short hair wearing a red coat, standing on a city street with cars in the background.
